name: Your Trainer Workout (.ytw)
spec: The Your Trainer Workout format is a JSON document. Top level: {"format": "ytw", "version": 1, "name": str, "description": str, "author": str, "steps": [..]}. Power is a fraction of FTP (1.0 == FTP), matching ZWO/MRC semantics, so the file is FTP-independent. Each step has a "kind": warmup, cooldown, ramp (duration_s, power_low, power_high), steady (duration_s, power), interval (repeat, on_duration_s, off_duration_s, on_power, off_power), or freeride (duration_s). Any step may carry an optional integer "cadence" (rpm). .ytw imports directly into Your Trainer; it is the canonical authoring target of the build/decompose tools and round-trips losslessly with ZWO.

constraints.note: Power values are fractions of FTP; 1.0 == FTP.
format must equal "ytw" and version must be a supported integer (currently 1).
interval steps expand to repeat on/off pairs in document order.

conversion_notes.note: 1:1 step mapping to Warmup/Cooldown/Ramp/SteadyState/IntervalsT/FreeRide; power fractions unchanged.
Multiply power fractions by 100 for %FTP rows; sample ramps/intervals into minute,percent points.
Encode power fractions as FIT %FTP (1000+percent); expand intervals/ramps to discrete time steps.

glossary.definition: Target as a fraction of FTP (1.0 == FTP).
